news.t: King Cash Is Being Dethroned. What to Buy Now.
Best Low-Volatility ETFs for When the Market is a Roller Coaster

overview.description: The Vanguard Short-Term Bond ETF (BSV) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in investment grade fixed income. The fund tracks a market-weighted index of US-government bonds, investment-grade corporate and investment-grade international dollar-denominated bonds with maturities of 1-5 years. BSV was launched on Apr 3, 2007 and is issued by Vanguard.
infoTable: Bloomberg US Government/Credit - Float Adjusted (1-5 Y)

data.title: King Cash Is Being Dethroned. What to Buy Now.
data.text: Consider bond ETFs from Vanguard, dividends, and growth stocks as cash moves off the sidelines.

data.title: Best Low-Volatility ETFs for When the Market is a Roller Coaster
data.text: It's been a volatile few years for the stock market, and that has investors sniffing out low-volatility ETFs. Just understand their strengths and their limitations.

data.title: Here's why some fund managers expect a bond resurgence
data.text: Bonds in portfolios have disappointed many investors over the past decade, but some fund managers expect a big resurgence. CNBC's Kristina Partsinevelos joins 'Squawk on the Street' with the details.
